title: Tapping for Health Anxiety
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the checking loop that does not stay quiet
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for health anxiety: the checking that quiets it temporarily, the catastrophising that runs fast, and the exhaustion of monitoring the body too closely. The script works on the loop rather than the specific fear.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the health-monitoring loop is active and reassurance is not landing. Good after a medical check when the reassurance has worn off quickly.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the health-anxiety loop. The checking quiets it temporarily. Then it returns. The script works on the loop itself: monitoring, escalating, seeking reassurance, returning to the start. Not any specific symptom.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I keep checking and I cannot seem to stop, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the worry about my health is running in the background all the time, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I know it is probably nothing and the worry does not stop, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** The health anxiety is back.
**Side of eye:** I keep checking because the checking quiets it.
**Under eye:** Until it does not, and I check again.
**Under nose:** I notice every symptom.
**Chin:** Something small turns into a catastrophe very quickly.
**Collarbone:** The worry does not stay gone with reassurance.
**Under arm:** I am tired of monitoring my own body this closely.
**Top of head:** The anxiety is more exhausting than the thing I am afraid of.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** The anxiety is a feeling, not a diagnosis.
**Side of eye:** I can notice a symptom without running a verdict.
**Under eye:** Most of what I have worried about has not happened.
**Under nose:** I can let this thought pass without acting on it.
**Chin:** My body is doing its job. The anxiety is the signal, not the problem.
**Collarbone:** I do not need certainty to feel safe.
**Under arm:** I can put down the monitoring for a few hours.
**Top of head:** I can trust my body. I can trust myself.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
